title: pouteria stipulifera
intro: Pouteria stipulifera, also known as the Brazilian cherry or guanabara, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is a small tree with a broad, rounded crown and a straight, smooth trunk, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The leaves and bark of the Pouteria stipulifera plant are used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health problems.
